title: transaction — authorization policies
description: Two policies, one per owned model. WRITE actions on Transaction + TransactionLedgerEntry are NEVER exposed to HTTP — transactions are recorded ONLY through internal service calls (TransactionRecorder) by invoice / payment / refund / chargeback modules. The one exception is the reversal endpoint, which creates a NEW offsetting transaction (never mutates). Ledger entries are ALWAYS internal — no HTTP surface for them at all.

abilities.scope: admin + finance role only. Support role has cross-tenant view via the platform plane, NOT the tenant plane.
admin + finance role. Same scoping as viewAny.

transactions are recorded ONLY via TransactionRecorder from invoice / payment / refund / chargeback modules. Direct HTTP create is refused with `TRANSACTION_MANUAL_CREATE_FORBIDDEN` at the route layer (there is no POST /transactions endpoint; the policy exists to defend against a future misconfigured route).
transactions are IMMUTABLE after post. TransactionObserver.updating refuses at the model layer; this policy is defense-in-depth.
transactions are append-only. Hard-purge only via PurgeExpiredTransactionDataJob (after archive to compliance-storage).

admin ONLY (finance role does NOT hold reverse). Every reversal creates a new offsetting transaction — the original stays immutable. The reversal endpoint requires a written note + writes to audits with 7-year retention.
same audience as view — admin + finance role.
admin + finance role. Ledger entries are the audit-critical row-level record — every read is logged.

ledger entries are created ONLY by TransactionRecorder inside the parent transaction's atomic DB transaction. Every entry has exactly one point of entry: the transaction's creating hook. There is no HTTP surface.
ledger entries are IMMUTABLE (append-only). Corrections happen via new reversal transactions.
hard-purge cascades from PurgeExpiredTransactionDataJob via FK CASCADE after the parent transaction is archived.
platform_ops — cross-tenant ledger-integrity investigations during audits
